---
title: "Pre-Hackathon Tasks - `r params$proj_name`"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
engine: knitr
params:
  proj_name: "Project name"
  proj_desc: "Project description"
  proj_important: "This project is important because"
  proj_solution: "This project will solve the problem by"
  proj_data_1: "Data needed for the project..."
  proj_data_2: "Data availability..."
  proj_code: "Existing code available..."
  proj_tools: "Tools to use for the project..."
  proj_know: "A subject knowledge expert will/will not be available"
  proj_real: "Realistic expectations for the project..."
  proj_chall: "Known challenges and limitations..."
  proj_info: "Other information about the project..."
  proj_datacamp: "Data camp courses you can use ..."
  proj_other_resources: "Other resources you can use ..."
  proj_skills: "Skills you will develop by working on this project"
  proj_repo: "GitHub repository link"
---


## Pre-Hackathon Tasks Checklist

-   [ ] MS Teams

-   [ ]  GitHub

-   [ ] Learning Resources

## MS Teams

-   We created a MS Teams channel for you to ask questions and get help from the organisers and volunteers.
-   Turn on the notifications for the 'General' channel in the Pre-stats awayday hackathon group on MS Teams to get updates about the hackathon before and during the event as shown below.

![](images/teams_notifs.png)

## Code sharing - GitHub

::: callout-tip
No experience using Git or would like a refresher? Use [Git Academy](https://dfe-analytical-services.github.io/git-academy/), a self-directed training resource to help upskill in Git.
:::

GitHub will be the primary platform for collaborating on code, managing project files, and tracking progress during the Hackathon because it allows for:

-   **Collaboration**: Work together in real time, even if you're in different locations.
-   **Version Control**: Keep track of changes, revert to previous versions, and avoid overwriting each other’s work.
-   **Transparency**: Everyone can see what’s being worked on and contribute where needed.
-   **Documentation**: Use the README file in your repo and issues to explain your project and track tasks.
-   **Showcase and share your work**: At the end of the Hackathon, your GitHub repo becomes a portfolio piece you can share with the team that proposed the project.

#### Your GitHub Repo

-   Create an account if you don’t have one yet. - [GitHub Sign Up](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/start-your-journey/creating-an-account-on-github#signing-up-for-a-new-personal-account).
-   [Email Statistics.DEVELOPMENT\@education.gov.uk](mailto:Statistics.DEVELOPMENT@education.gov.uk) with your GitHub username you can be given access to the repos.
-   Use [Git Academy](https://dfe-analytical-services.github.io/git-academy/), a self-directed training resource to help upskill in Git.


## Online courses and resources to help upskill

### DataCamp

::: callout-tip
Don't have a DataCamp license? Email Lisa Smyth at [datacamp.requests\@education.gov.uk](mailto:datacamp.requests@education.gov.uk) ccing your line manager to get your license before the hackathon!
:::

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}

if (params$proj_datacamp == "") {
  cat("No DataCamp courses were provided for this project.\n")
} else {

sen <- stringr::str_split_1(params$proj_datacamp, ";")

  for (i in sen){
    
    cat("- ", i,"\n")
  }}
```

### Other resources

```{r other_resources , results='asis', echo=FALSE}

if (isTRUE(nzchar(params$proj_other_resources))) {
  sen <- stringr::str_split_1(params$proj_other_resources, ";")
  for (i in sen) {
    cat("- ", i, "\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("No other resources were provided for this project.\n")
}



```
